titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 70

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=130 status=live modified=2018-07-31 01:55:06.485541+00 | translation moa=131 status=live modified=2016-06-07 15:04:23.282372+00

div.p:
Sancte Matris ecclesię filiis tam futuris quam presentibus  Dunecanus Comes de fif  salutem . Sciant tam futuri quam
presentes me dedisse & concessisse & hac mea carta confirmasse deo & ecclesie sancti Andreę apostoli
& canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus & seruituris capellam de chatel cum terra & cum decimis cum obla
tionibus & cum omnibus ecclesie capellę pertinentibus tenendam & possidendam libere & quiete
& absque reclamatione heredum meorum perpetuo in elemosinam . Quare uolo ut predicti canonici pre
nominatm elemosinam habeant & possideant ita libere & quiete pro salute animę meę & animabus antecesso
rum & successorum meorum  sicut liberius & quiecius tenent & possident ceteras elemosinas suas in reg
no scotię . Testibus . Michaele clerico . Stephano clerico . hugone clerico comitis . helia clerico de benna .
Willelmo capellano de chilrimund’ . Odone dapifer . Willelmo de holdernesse . Ricardo Malluuel . Mi
lone homine comitis . Rogero de thorp.

To all sons of Holy Mother Church, future as well as present, Duncan Earl of Fife sends Greeting: Let future as well as present know that I have given and granted and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Andrew the apostle and the canons serving God in that place, now and in the future, the chapel of Kettle, with land and with teinds, with oblations and with all things pertaining to the church of the chapel, to be held and possessed freely and quietly and without recovery by my heirs, in perpetual alms. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid canons should have and possess the aforenamed alms as freely and quietly, for the salvation of my soul and the souls of my ancestors and successors, as they hold and possess the rest of their alms in the kingdom of Scotia. As witnesses: Michael the clerk; Stephen the clerk; Hugh the earl’s clerk; Elias the clerk of Benholm; William the chaplain of Kirlymont; Odo the steward; William of Holderness; Richard Melville; Milo the earl’s man; Roger of Thorpe.
